steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens: ‘A painting by 19th-century artist Katsushika Hokusai,’ — an introductory noun phrase followed by a comma. The main clause that follows must have that painting as its subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — makes ‘meaning’ the subject: breaks the grammatical link from the introductory phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — also makes ‘meaning’ the subject: same error as A. ✗
  • Choice C — makes ‘visual allegory’ the subject: still doesn’t continue from the introduction of the painting. ✗
  • Choice D — makes ‘The Great Wave off Kanagawa’ the subject: the painting itself is the subject of the main clause, correctly completing the introductory phrase. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
